perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. The Receiving Party is allowed a 90 day grace period to remove all copies of the Confidential Information, once the request has been made. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a 90-day grace period for removing copies of Confidential Information. This contradicts the original 'promptly deliver' requirement upon written notice, creating ambiguity regarding the actual deadline for returning or destroying the information. The 'promptly deliver' statement causes the in-text contradiction. This creates an inconsistency in the receiving party's obligations, as 'promptly' is being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. The Receiving Party is allowed a 90 day grace period to remove all copies of the Confidential Information, once the request has been made. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 90-day grace period for removing confidential information, directly contradicting the original requirement to 'promptly deliver' upon written notice. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the actual deadline for returning or destroying the information, therefore the two sections are contradictory. The contradicted text and changed text are the same in this case, demonstrating the direct impact of the change.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. This agreement automatically terminates after 6 months, irrespective of Confidential Information disclosed or if the aforementioned events take place. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement automatically terminates after 6 months, regardless of the other conditions mentioned (formal agreement or Confidential Information disclosed). This directly contradicts the original clause that allows the agreement to continue for a year or until a formal agreement is reached, creating ambiguity. The initial statement of the Agreement is creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. This agreement automatically terminates after 6 months, irrespective of Confidential Information disclosed or if the aforementioned events take place. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the agreement lasts for one year or until a formal agreement is reached. The changed text adds that the agreement automatically terminates after 6 months irrespective of other factors. This directly conflicts with the possibility of the agreement lasting a year as stated in the contradicted text. The contradicted text is the exact text of the original text and shows that there is a contradiction because the change limits the agreement to only 6 months, breaking the one year agreement stated previously.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall inure to the benefit of, the Parties and their respective representatives, successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall inure to the benefit of, the Parties. However, this agreement is not transferrable to any successors or assigns without the written consent of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement is not transferrable to successors or assigns without written consent. This directly contradicts the original clause that states the agreement 'shall be binding upon, and shall inure to the benefit of, the Parties and their respective representatives, successors and assigns.' It undermines the certainty that the agreement will continue if one of the parties were to be acquired or merge, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall inure to the benefit of, the Parties. However, this agreement is not transferrable to any successors or assigns without the written consent of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the agreement benefits successors and assigns. The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is not transferable to successors or assigns without written consent. This direct conflict in the transferability of the agreement to successors and assigns creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, confirming that the changed text is the source of the contradiction. As per the provided explanation, the change undermines the certainty of the agreement's continuation upon acquisition or merger, leading to potential disputes and enforceability issues. This satisfies the definition of an Inconsistency - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes any prior oral or written agreements, and all contemporaneous oral communications. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by the Parties. Any failure to enforce a provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes any prior oral or written agreements, and all contemporaneous oral communications. All additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made orally and shall be binding, regardless of whether they are signed. Any failure to enforce a provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that all additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made orally, this creating an in-text contradiction with the prior statment of all modifications having to be in writing and signed by both parties, and shall be binding, regardless of whether they are signed. The in-text contradiction undermines the certainty and clarity of the agreement, as it is now unclear how modifications are to be made. This leads to potential disputes regarding whether oral agreements are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes any prior oral or written agreements, and all contemporaneous oral communications. All additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made orally and shall be binding, regardless of whether they are signed. Any failure to enforce a provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself by stating that modifications must be in writing and signed, and then immediately states they can be oral and binding regardless of signature. This is a direct and clear conflict and thus creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the given definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text here, which does not mean there is no contradiction. It means the contradiction occurs within the changed section, which the explanation outlines.
